docs.title: Enterprise QE Academy
docs.text: Enterprise QE Academy is a practical learning and interview-preparation platform for Enterprise Quality Engineering, SDET, Automation Architect, Data QA, Test Management, and QE Architecture roles.

docs.title: Lead QE / Test Manager Path
docs.text:
1. SQL & Data Validation
  2. API Testing
  3. Automation Frameworks
  4. Banking or Healthcare Domain Pack
  5. Whiteboarding
  6. Behavioral STAR practice

docs.title: SDET / Automation Architect Path
docs.text:
1. Java Practice
  2. API Testing
  3. Selenium / Playwright
  4. CI/CD and GitHub Actions
  5. Framework Architecture
  6. Whiteboarding

docs.title: Data QA Engineer Path
docs.text:
1. SQL & Data Validation
  2. Data Quality & ETL
  3. Cloud & DevOps
  4. Healthcare or Banking Data Scenarios
  5. Evidence Packages

docs.title: Search Examples
docs.text: Use the site search box for SQL reconciliation, masking, FHIR, payments, Playwright, Snowflake, whiteboard, or API contract testing.

docs.title: v1
docs.text: Searchable catalog, Java sample labs, MkDocs site, CI.

docs.title: v2
docs.text: 5,000+ questions, SQL labs, API labs, Selenium/Playwright labs.

docs.title: v3
docs.text: Adaptive practice engine, timed assessments, scorecards.

docs.title: v4
docs.text: Career Compiler integration, resume project mapping, interview scoring.

docs.title: Local CLI Search
docs.text:
python scripts/search_catalog.py --keyword masking --limit 10
python scripts/search_catalog.py --category SQL_DATA_VALIDATION --difficulty Hard
python scripts/search_catalog.py --domain Banking --role "Lead Quality Engineer"

docs.title: Static Site Search
docs.text: Use the built-in MkDocs search box in the left navigation.

docs.title: Skill Coverage
docs.text:
• Java, Python, SQL
  • Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, Tosca
  • REST Assured, Postman, ReadyAPI
  • Snowflake, Databricks, PySpark, Informatica, Talend
  • Jenkins, GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps
  • Banking, payments, lending, settlement, reconciliation
  • FACETS, MMIS, claims, EDI X12, FHIR, HL7
  • Retail OMS/WMS/TMS, supply chain, Blue Yonder
  • AWS, Azure, Docker, Kubernetes, observability
  • AI/LLM validation and RAG testing

docs.title: AI Quality Engineering
docs.text: AI QE focuses on validating LLM behavior, prompt quality, retrieval grounding, hallucination risk, test data generation, and AI-assisted quality workflows.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• LLM validation
  • prompt testing
  • RAG evaluation
  • hallucination detection
  • grounding verification
  • synthetic test data
  • AI defect triage
  • AI-assisted reconciliation
  • agentic workflow validation

docs.title: Generated Examples
docs.text:
• AI QE Example 1
  • AI QE Example 2

docs.title: API Testing Practice
docs.text: API testing validates service behavior, contract stability, integration correctness, security, and reliability across microservices and enterprise platforms.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• REST API testing
  • SOAP service validation
  • Postman collections
  • ReadyAPI / SOAPUI
  • REST Assured automation
  • Contract testing
  • Schema validation
  • OAuth and authentication
  • Negative testing
  • Error handling
  • Idempotency and retries

docs.title: REST Assured Sketch
docs.text:
given()
    .baseUri(baseUrl)
    .header("Authorization", "Bearer " + token)
.when()
    .get("/payments/{id}", paymentId)
.then()
    .statusCode(200)
    .body("status", notNullValue());

docs.title: Cypress Practice
docs.text: Cypress is useful for frontend component and end-to-end validation where fast feedback and developer-friendly debugging are important.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• test runner
  • fixtures
  • intercepts
  • network stubbing
  • component testing
  • screenshots and videos
  • CI execution
  • dashboard reporting

docs.title: Playwright Practice
docs.text: Playwright is valuable for modern UI automation, API+UI testing, browser tracing, and stable end-to-end testing.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• fixtures
  • locators
  • traces
  • screenshots
  • retries
  • API + UI combined testing
  • parallel execution
  • browser contexts
  • CI integration

docs.text:

  • CI/CD Quality Gate Example

docs.title: Selenium Practice
docs.text: Selenium practice focuses on enterprise UI automation, maintainable frameworks, locator strategy, synchronization, reporting, and CI/CD execution.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• Page Object Model
  • waits and synchronization
  • locator strategy
  • cross-browser testing
  • Selenium Grid
  • parallel execution
  • screenshots and evidence
  • flaky test triage
  • reusable utilities
  • CI/CD integration

docs.title: Catalog Browser
docs.text:
The academy currently contains 10,080 catalog records.

The full question database lives in:

catalog/questions.jsonl
catalog/questions.csv

This MkDocs site intentionally uses lightweight catalog browse pages instead of generating one HTML page per question. That keeps the site fast and prevents local disk/build overload.

docs.title: Online IDE and Coding Practice
docs.text: GitHub Pages is a static site, so it cannot execute Java, Python, SQL, or Playwright code directly.

docs.title: Recommended Now
docs.text:
Use GitHub Codespaces:

  1. Open the GitHub repo.
  2. Click Code.
  3. Click Codespaces.
  4. Create a new Codespace.
  5. Run practice commands in the browser terminal.

docs.title: Local VS Code
docs.text:
cd C:\GitHub\Enterprise_QE_Academy_Consumable_GitSolution\enterprise_qe_academy_consumable_solution
code .

docs.title: Java Practice
docs.text: Java practice prepares you for coding rounds, whiteboarding, and automation-framework implementation.

docs.title: Core Concepts
docs.text:
• Collections: HashMap, LinkedHashMap, HashSet, LinkedHashSet, ArrayList, Deque
  • String algorithms: parsing, masking, normalization, tokenization
  • Algorithmic patterns: sorting, searching, sliding window, two pointers, recursion
  • Testing: JUnit 5, TestNG, assertions, edge cases
  • Production-readiness: null safety, deterministic outputs, readable code, complexity analysis

docs.title: Duplicate Detection
docs.text: Find duplicate values while preserving the order of first appearance.

docs.title: First Unique Character
docs.text: Find the first non-repeating character in a string.

docs.title: Mask Sensitive Data
docs.text: Mask all digits except the last four while preserving separators.

cd java\core
mvn clean test

docs.title: Practice Engine
docs.text: The Practice Engine supports coherent tutor-style interview preparation.

docs.title: Recommended Tutor Session
docs.text:
python scripts\start_practice_session.py --role "Lead Quality Engineer" --domain Banking --difficulty Hard --count 5 --mode tutor

This creates a Markdown report under:

practice/reports/

Each question includes:

  • question prompt
  • Hint 1
  • Hint 2
  • model answer
  • follow-up questions
  • scorecard
  • improvement notes

docs.title: Export to ChatGPT Tutor Prompt
docs.text:
python scripts\export_chatgpt_prompt.py --report practice\reports\session_YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS_tutor.md

Then start a new ChatGPT thread and paste the generated prompt.

docs.text: python scripts\reveal_solution.py --id SQL-08230 --level solution

  3. Future enhancement: React + FastAPI + Monaco editor.

docs.title: AI_-00016 â€” RAG evaluation for SaaS CI/CD quality gate adoption
Metadata
docs.text:
• Difficulty: Hard
  • Category: AI_QE
  • Concept: RAG evaluation
  • Domain: SaaS
  • Role: QE Architect
  • Tags: ai-qe, rag-evaluation, saas, qe-architect, hard

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text: Design, implement, test, or whiteboard a hard solution involving RAG evaluation for SaaS CI/CD quality gate adoption. Explain assumptions, edge cases, validation strategy, quality risks, evidence expected, complexity, and production-readiness considerations.

docs.title: Clarifying Questions
docs.text:
• What are the expected input formats?
  • What should happen for null, empty, duplicate, malformed, or high-volume data?
  • What evidence is required to prove correctness?

docs.title: Solution Strategy
docs.text:
• Clarify expected input/output and business meaning.
  • Identify failure modes and data/automation risks.
  • Propose a simple baseline approach.
  • Optimize for deterministic behavior, maintainability, and observability.
  • Add unit/API/SQL/UI tests depending on the skill category.
  • Capture terminal or CI evidence.
  • Explain time/space complexity or operational complexity.
  • Map the concept to an enterprise project scenario.

docs.title: Test Evidence Expected
docs.text:
• Unit test or executable validation
  • Terminal output
  • CI build result
  • Edge-case coverage summary

docs.title: Interview Defense
docs.text: Explain the selected data structure or testing strategy, trade-offs, complexity, and how this applies to modernization programs.

docs.title: SQL and Data Validation Practice
docs.text: SQL is essential for Enterprise QE, Data QA, ETL testing, healthcare payer validation, and banking reconciliation work.

docs.title: Core Topics
docs.text:
• Source-to-target validation
  • Duplicate detection
  • Join-based reconciliation
  • Missing record checks
  • Count, sum, and control-total validation
  • Window functions
  • Grouping and aggregation
  • Null handling
  • Data profiling

docs.title: Duplicate Business Keys
docs.text:
SELECT business_key, COUNT(*) AS record_count
FROM target_table
GROUP BY business_key
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;

docs.title: Source-to-Target Count Check
docs.text:
SELECT 'source' AS layer, COUNT(*) AS row_count
FROM source_table
UNION ALL
SELECT 'target' AS layer, COUNT(*) AS row_count
FROM target_table;

docs.title: Missing Records
docs.text:
SELECT business_key FROM source_table
EXCEPT
SELECT business_key FROM target_table;

docs.text:
• Domain: Banking
  • Role: Lead Quality Engineer
  • Difficulty: Hard

Explain and defend a production-ready PII strategy for Banking as a Lead Quality Engineer.

docs.title: Model answer excerpt
docs.text:
I would start by clarifying the business workflow, data grain, upstream/downstream systems, and release risk for PII in a Banking account onboarding scenario. Because this is a hard Lead Quality Engineer interview problem, I would show lead-level ownership, cross-team coordination, automation strategy, risk-based prioritization, and release evidence.

My solution would focus on data protection, least privilege, auditability, masking, encryption, retention, consent, and regulatory evidence. I would define the acceptance criteria first, map them to testable controls, and separate validation into unit/component, API or integration, data, security, performance, and UAT evidence. For automation, I would create reusable checks that run in CI/CD, tag tests by risk and domain, and publish results with traceable evidence.

  • Difficulty: Hard
  • Quality score: 100/100

Explain and defend a production-ready Joins strategy for Banking as a Lead Quality Engineer.

docs.title: Model answer excerpt
docs.text:
I would start by clarifying the business workflow, data grain, upstream/downstream systems, and release risk for Joins in a Banking account onboarding scenario. Because this is a hard Lead Quality Engineer interview problem, I would show lead-level ownership, cross-team coordination, automation strategy, risk-based prioritization, and release evidence.

My solution would focus on source-to-target validation, reconciliation, schema drift, data quality rules, lineage, and recoverability. I would define the acceptance criteria first, map them to testable controls, and separate validation into unit/component, API or integration, data, security, performance, and UAT evidence. For automation, I would create reusable checks that run in CI/CD, tag tests by risk and domain, and publish results with traceable evidence.

For production readiness, I would require clear pass/fail gates, defect triage...

docs.text:
flowchart LR
  A[Source systems] --> B[Ingestion]
  B --> C[Validation rules]
  C --> D[Curated data]
  C --> E[Exception queue]
  D --> F[Analytics / downstream APIs]
  C --> G[Quality evidence]

docs.title: How to use this dashboard
docs.text:
• Use this page before publishing new enterprise packs.
  • Treat fail scenarios as blockers for premium/interview-ready content.
  • Treat review scenarios as acceptable for internal practice but not final curated packs.
  • Expand short or placeholder answers before scaling the catalog.

docs.title: v4 Quality Intelligence Layer
docs.text: The Quality Intelligence Layer turns Enterprise QE Academy v4 from a content generator into a governed learning platform.

docs.title: What it adds
docs.text:
• Automated knowledge-node quality scoring
  • Placeholder and shallow-answer detection
  • Rubric validation
  • Evidence/readiness validation
  • Static GitHub Pages dashboard generation
  • GitHub Actions artifact workflow
  • One-command v4 release builder

docs.title: Local commands
docs.text:
python .\scripts\build_v4_release.py --domain Banking --role "Lead Quality Engineer" --difficulty Hard --limit 250

Or run each step manually:

python .\scripts\generate_v4_platform_core.py --domain Banking --role "Lead Quality Engineer" --difficulty Hard --limit 250
python .\scripts\generate_v4_portal_pages.py
python .\scripts\assess_v4_quality.py
python .\scripts\generate_v4_quality_dashboard.py

docs.title: Generated outputs
docs.text:
• outputs/v4/quality_report.json
  • outputs/v4/quality_report.md
  • outputs/v4/quality_gate_status.json
  • docs/docs/v4/quality-dashboard.md

docs.title: Recommended gate policy
docs.text:
Use the gate as a warning initially. Once content quality improves, run:

python .\scripts\assess_v4_quality.py --fail-on-gate

This can later become a required GitHub Actions quality gate before publishing curated packs.

docs.text: The v5 interview engine runs as a static GitHub Pages browser app.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• One-question-at-a-time mock interview flow.

  • Answer capture with local autosave.
  • Hint 1, Hint 2, model answer, follow-up questions, and self-critique checklist.
  • 100-point Enterprise QE scoring rubric.
  • Question navigation and mark-for-review.
  • Timer, progress metrics, readiness label, and completion summary.
  • Copy-to-ChatGPT interviewer prompt.
  • Exportable Markdown session summary.
